feed.title: NASA
feed.subtitle: Combined NASA feed: the main newsroom, the blogs firehose, NASA Science, the launch schedule, the Image of the Day, and Astronomy Picture of the Day.

entry.title: Fans of the Arctic
entry.content: Sediment eroded from ice-capped mountains splays out across a broad river valley on Russia’s Severny Island.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4941-4947: (Pin)Stripes on the Fourth of July
entry.content: Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Thursday, July 2, 2026 Curiosity spent the week leading up to the Fourth of July holiday approaching a geologic boundary between a very smooth but somewhat sandy region and a rougher bedrock unit. Leaving the polygonal […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
entry.content: No one wants to get into an uncomfortable aircraft. NASA research could help the emerging industry of air taxis —small, vertical-takeoff-and-landing aircraft meant for short trips — understand the relationship between comfort and willingness to fly. That’s where NASA comes in, with data that can help identify how to plan air taxi rides that can […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s SunRISE Mission Changes Launch Vehicle to SpaceX Falcon Heavy
entry.content: NASA’s SunRISE (Sun Radio Interferometer Space Experiment) mission will launch on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, shifting from its original ride into space aboard a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur vehicle. NASA will share updated launch timing in the near future. The heliophysics mission will fly as […] The post NASA’s SunRISE Mission Changes Launch Vehicle to SpaceX Falcon Heavy appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Roman Workshop, 13-16 July 2026
entry.content: Shifting Landscapes in Astrophysics New Frontiers to Explore with Roman The post Roman Workshop, 13-16 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Artemis III Flight Hardware Stacks Up at Kennedy
entry.content: It is full steam ahead at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida as teams process and assemble flight hardware in preparation for the upcoming crewed Artemis III mission. Less than three months after the safe return of the Artemis II crew, teams began stacking the twin SLS (Space Launch System) solid rocket booster segments inside […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Expedition 74 Stays Busy and Awaits Tuesday’s New Crew Arrival
entry.content: Expedition 74 is poised to welcome three new crew members to the International Space Station who are counting down to their launch aboard a Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Solar System Exploration Research Virtual Institute (SSERVI) Cooperative Agreement Notice
entry.content: We are currently seeking reviewers for the Solar System Exploration Research Virtual Institute (SSERVI) Cooperative Agreement Notice. Here are the rules: The post Solar System Exploration Research Virtual Institute (SSERVI) Cooperative Agreement Notice appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon poses in a spacesuit for a portrait at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas on Jan. 8, 2026. Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
entry.content: Astronomers using archival data from Hubble and supportive observations from Webb have located their first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Debris Disk Connections
entry.content: Date: July 13, 2026 - July 17, 2026 Location: University of Cambridge The post Debris Disk Connections appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Wild, Scenic, and Increasingly Rusty
entry.content: Orange streams are now being spotted in hundreds of watersheds in permafrost areas throughout Alaska’s Brooks Range. The post Wild, Scenic, and Increasingly Rusty appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Galaxy NGC 474: Shells and Star Streams
entry.content:
What's happening to galaxy NGC 474?
The multiple layers of emission appear strangely complex given the relatively featureless appearance of the elliptical galaxy in less deep images
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Messier 24: Sagittarius Star Cloud
entry.content: Unlike most entries in Charles Messier's famous catalog of deep sky objects, M24 is not a bright galaxy, star cluster, or nebula
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: NASA CubeSat to Speed Technology Testing in Orbit
entry.content: The R5-S9 (Realizing Rapid, Reduced-cost high-Risk Research project Spacecraft 9) CubeSat, developed by NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, lifted off at 12:12 a.m. PDT Tuesday, July 7, aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 4 East at Vandenberg Space Force Base in California as part of the Transporter-17 rideshare mission with SpaceX. […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Freedom 250 Flies High at NASA
entry.content: Enjoy this collection of NASA aircraft images honoring the United States' 250th birthday in 2026. The post Freedom 250 Flies High at NASA appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
entry.content: Back to ECF Home Advanced Diagnostics for High-Enthalpy Test Facilities Simulating Spacecraft Atmospheric Entry Planning for Autonomous Spacecraft Using Machine Learning Methods to Enable Onboard Guidance, Navigation, and Control
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Calibration Instrument Records First Measurements in Space
entry.content: NASA’s Calibration Absolute Radiance and Refractivity Observatory Pathfinder, or CLARREO Pathfinder mission, reached a significant milestone this summer by achieving first light. First light showcases a mission’s ability to successfully power on and capture data, in this case detailed spectral data, validating years of preparation and pre-launch testing. This moment marks the first time the […] The post NASA Calibration Instrument Records First Measurements in Space appeared first on NASA Scienc
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
entry.content: The Zooniverse, a NASA grantee that runs the world’s largest platform for online people-powered research, has reached an extraordinary milestone: 1 billion classifications contributed by volunteers around the world. This milestone is a celebration of everyone who has marked a dip in a light curve, confirmed the presence of a moving object in a short […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Viking: 50 Years on Mars
entry.content: Only 10 years before the Viking mission launched, Mars was still just a mottled orange ball when viewed through even the best Earthbound telescopes, with few discernable features. Three NASA missions flew past in the 1960s, snagging grainy snapshots of a sliver of Mars as they hurtled by. In 1971, the Mariner 9 spacecraft arrived, […] The post Viking: 50 Years on Mars appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Earthrise, Earthset: Looking Towards Home
entry.content: Views of Earth from human & robotic explorers on their way to, around, and back home from the Moon. The post Earthrise, Earthset: Looking Towards Home appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Moonscapes
entry.content: See the Moon's mountains, craters, lava flows, and more through the "eyes" of robotic explorers like NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). The post Moonscapes appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Revealing the Invisible
entry.content: These Moon data maps reveal lunar surface temperatures, slope and elevation, subsurface rockiness, and more. The post Revealing the Invisible appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: The Moon from Earth
entry.content: Images of eclipses, supermoons, and more, captured by photographers right here on Earth. The post The Moon from Earth appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Views from the Lunar Surface
entry.content: POV: you're on the Moon. These are images captured by human and robotic visitors to the lunar surface. The post Views from the Lunar Surface appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Moon Image Galleries
entry.content: The first Earthrise picture snapped over the lunar horizon. Artemis II Moon photos. Decades of views from robotic explorers. Find top Moon images here. The post Moon Image Galleries appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
entry.content: NASA flight photographers capture history from a perspective few ever experience, getting a rare bird’s-eye view of the agency’s missions in action. Their photos document key NASA research and give the public a front-row seat to the work happening behind the scenes. Jim Ross, a photographer at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Beta Pictoris d
entry.content: Beta Pictoris d is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a A-type star. Its mass is 2.4 Jupiters, it takes 91 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 26 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post Beta Pictoris d appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-6019 b
entry.content: TOI-6019 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a G-type star. Its mass is 0.46880643 Jupiters, it takes 14.5 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.1212 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-6019 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-2147 b
entry.content: TOI-2147 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 0.36497682 Jupiters, it takes 26.2 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.172 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-2147 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Week Ends With Vein Scans, Cartilage Engineering Before New Crew Launches
entry.content: Vein scans and cartilage engineering wrapped up a busy week aboard the International Space Station. The Expedition 74 crew members also configured the orbital outpost for a new crew, tested new cooking gear, and conducted muscle measurements.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
entry.content: The waxing gibbous moon is nestled in the darkness of space in this June 26, 2026, image from the International Space Station. The space station was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar at the time. The waxing gibbous phase comes before the full moon phase. During this time, the Moon appears brighter […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NGTS-38
entry.content: The post NGTS-38 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-6019
entry.content: The post TOI-6019 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-2147
entry.content: The post TOI-2147 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: On-Orbit Checkouts in Progress for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: Commissioning is well underway for Katalyst’s robotic servicing spacecraft LINK, which is designed to boost the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. The post On-Orbit Checkouts in Progress for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Space Runs in the Family
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon discusses his journey ahead of his first spaceflight to the International Space Station aboard Soyuz MS-29. Episode 428. The post Space Runs in the Family appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Western Moon, Eastern Sea
entry.content: The Mare Orientale, Latin for Eastern Sea, is one of the most striking large scale lunar features
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
entry.content: Radar data from the NISAR satellite show that La Guaira and nearby areas experienced significant ground displacement from the June 2026 temblors.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Video: The LAST Eclipse in History
entry.content: We are in the Golden Age of Solar Eclipses, but only for the moment. In fact, we may already be past peak solar eclipse conditions, and it's all downhill from here. The post Video: The LAST Eclipse in History appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Video: Which Planet Has the Best Eclipse?
entry.content: Solar eclipses don't just happen here on earth - moons of other planets also pass between those planets and the Sun, resulting in various types of solar eclipses across the solar system. The post Video: Which Planet Has the Best Eclipse? appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Helio and You: June 2026
entry.content: As the only state to lie partly within the Arctic Circle, Alaska experiences a wide range of extreme conditions. But with these extremes comes the chance to conduct science vital to understanding the Sun-Earth system, including launching sounding rockets to study solar flares, auroras, and other phenomena. This article explores the ways that scientists use […] The post Helio and You: June 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition 74 crew advancing scientific research. Menon, Dubrov, and Kikina will lift off at 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: AGN SIG Spotlight Series, 21 July 2026
entry.content: Our Spotlight Series highlights recent advances in AGN science, with a strong emphasis on participation from early-career researchers, and includes plenty of time for community discussion following the presentations. The post AGN SIG Spotlight Series, 21 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
entry.content: For the first time, scientists have used NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) to directly measure the magnetic fields of PSR J1101−6101, a pulsar located within what is often referred to as the Lighthouse Nebula. The results provide new insight into the structure of some of the most extreme objects in the cosmos, as NASA […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Roman Launch Preparations Proceed
entry.content: With less than two months until the launch of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, engineers at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida are preparing the observatory for its trip to space. Inside the Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility, technicians used large cranes to carefully lift Roman and place it onto a specialized work platform, known […] The post NASA’s Roman Launch Preparations Proceed appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Icing Research
entry.content: Recent News GlennICE Software Released on the NASA Software Repository (May 2025) New Aircraft Wing Undergoes Crucial NASA Icing Testing (March 25, 2025) An Inside Look: NASA’s Ice Tunnel (The Weather Channel – October 29, 2024) Glenn Celebrates 80 Years of Cool Aviation Research (2024) NASA Tunnel Generates Decades of Icy Aircraft Safety Data (September […] The post Icing Research appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Principal Investigator and Quality Assessment Reports Evaluate Umbra Synthetic Aperture Radar Data
entry.content: The reports add to the growing documentation on commercial data’s contributions to Earth science research and applications.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Neurology Studies and Crew Departure Preps Fill Station Schedule
entry.content: Neurology studies and crew departure preparations filled the schedule aboard the International Space Station on Thursday. Expedition 74 also kept up the maintenance of the orbital lab replacing electronics and GPS gear and organizing cargo.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: John Haynes
entry.content: Program Manager for Health and Air Quality Applications and the Multisource Integrated Observatory in the NASA Earth Action Program in the Earth Science Division at Headquarters The post John Haynes appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Dragonfly Clears Key Tests as Titan Rotorcraft Takes Shape
entry.content: NASA’s Dragonfly is starting to look less like a collection of spacecraft parts and more like the rotorcraft that will fly across the surface of Titan, Saturn’s hazy moon. The mission reached a major milestone on June 29, when the Dragonfly team at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, delivered the […] The post NASA’s Dragonfly Clears Key Tests as Titan Rotorcraft Takes Shape appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Erin Urquhart
entry.content: Program Manager for the NASA Earth Action Water Resources program within NASA’s Earth Science Division at Headquarters The post Erin Urquhart appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
entry.content: This close-up view shows fragments of sulfur crystals — the first ever seen on the Red Planet. The crystals were found after NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover happened to drive over a rock and crush it on May 30, 2024. Several days later, Curiosity used a camera on the end of its robotic arm to take […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Eleanor Stokes
entry.content: Dr. Eleanor Stokes serves as Program Manager for the new Earth Information Center at NASA Headquarters. The post Eleanor Stokes appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Dr. Ashutosh Limaye
entry.content: Dr. Ashutosh Limaye serves as the Program Manager for Agriculture at NASA Earth Action. The post Dr. Ashutosh Limaye appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
entry.content: This month, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California are testing a spacecraft sensor that will help measure how quickly Arctic sea ice is disappearing. And while that instrument won’t launch for another year, scientists started preparing for its use during a recent field campaign in the Canadian wilderness. Researchers spent two weeks […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Small Satellite Could Make Global Positioning More Precise
entry.content: GRITSS will demonstrate the feasibility of a new technique for improving precision navigation and geodetic measurements.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: The Red Glow of the Cosmic Bat Nebula
entry.content: This Cosmic Bat wishes you a happy Summerween! This mid-year celebration of Halloween transcends hemispheres, even though summer in the Northern hemisphere is winter in the South
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Super Typhoon Bavi
entry.content: The third category 5 tropical cyclone of 2026 crossed the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam before continuing toward Asia.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
entry.content: Story by Keri Moskowitz, Gulf of Maine Research Institute For the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation, or Sipayik, the ocean has always been a teacher. Situated in what is known as Downeast Maine, along the shores of Passamaquoddy Bay, generations of Indigenous people have lived along the coast, learning from the tides, the land, and their […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Crew Works Health Checks, CubeSat Maintenance, and Soyuz Seat Checks
entry.content: Health checks and CubeSat hardware maintenance were the prime research objectives aboard the International Space Station on Wednesday. The Expedition 74 crew members also stowed spacewalking gear, transferred cargo, and checked seats in a Soyuz spacecraft.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Small Steps, Giant Leaps Podcast Episode 177: Transformative Aeronautics
entry.content: A NASA research program collaborates with universities to revolutionize the way we design, build, and operate aircraft. Angela Surgenor, deputy program manager of the Transformative Aeronautics Concepts Program, explains. The post Small Steps, Giant Leaps Podcast Episode 177: Transformative Aeronautics appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: You’re Always Surrounded by Neutrinos!
entry.content: This second, as you’re reading these words, trillions of tiny particles are hurtling toward you! No, you don’t need to brace yourself. They’re passing through you right now. And now. And now. These particles are called neutrinos, and they’re both everywhere in the cosmos and also extremely hard to find. Neutrinos are fundamental particles, like […] The post You’re Always Surrounded by Neutrinos! appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Awards Contracts for Mars Advanced Surface Mobility Technology
entry.content: July 8, 2026 C.13 NASA Awards Contracts for Mars Advanced Surface Mobility Technology NASA has selected seven companies for contract awards under the Mars Exploration Program’s Science Transport and Robotic Innovation for Deployment and Exploration, or STRIDE, initiative to advance next-generation commercial robotic surface mobility for future Mars exploration. The STRIDE awards will support the […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
entry.content: This image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope showcases Messier 3 (M3), one of the Milky Way galaxy’s most massive globular clusters, or spherical collections of gravitationally bound stars. Globular clusters are made up of ancient stars that formed at roughly the same time from the same cloud of gas, giving those stars similar ages. Around 150 known globular clusters are […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah
entry.content: The blaze burned more than 150 square miles and swept through parts of a ski resort.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Harrison “Jack” Schmitt, Busy on the Moon (Apollo 17)
entry.content: Scientist-astronaut Harrison H. Schmitt is photographed working beside a huge boulder on the Moon. The post Harrison “Jack” Schmitt, Busy on the Moon (Apollo 17) appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Keeping PACE with Ocean Change
entry.content: The primary goal of this collaboration was to support and validate science data products from NASA's recently launched Plankton, Aerosol, Cloud, ocean Ecosystem (PACE) satellite mission The post Keeping PACE with Ocean Change appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Free Textbook on Deep Learning in Astrophysics
entry.content: Free Textbook on Deep Learning in Astrophysics Released by AI/ML STIG The post Free Textbook on Deep Learning in Astrophysics appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: SciENcv is Live
entry.content: SciENcv is Live; Required for All NSPIRES Proposals From 1 September 2026 The post SciENcv is Live appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: More Cartilage, Blood Pressure Studies on Station as Crew Swap Preps Begin
entry.content: Cartilage engineering and blood pressure studies to improve health on Earth and in space continued aboard the International Space Station on Tuesday. The Expedition 74 crew members also serviced an experimental module, configured a temporary sleep station, and began preparing for the return to Earth of three crewmates.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
entry.content: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of about 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center’s Greenbelt, Maryland, campus Tuesday to the adjoining Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The property, formerly known as NASA Goddard’s Area 400, is now part of the largest block of unfragmented forest between […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
entry.content: Following its longest hibernation period ever of nearly a year, NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft has emerged in good health and is ready to begin transmitting science data gathered in the distant Kuiper Belt far beyond Pluto. On June 23, flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, confirmed New Horizons, […] The post NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
entry.content: In this photograph, the Artemis II crew participates in the dedication of the Apollo 14 Moon tree at the Lunar Receiving Park at NASA’s Johnson Space Center on June 25, 2026. This tree is a second-generation Apollo Moon tree of the loblolly pine species. The original Apollo Moon trees were grown from seeds carried aboard […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
entry.content: Your challenge is to tell us the location of the satellite image and why it is interesting.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s COFFIES Science Center Makes Breakthrough on Solar Enigma
entry.content: Researchers working with one of NASA’s DRIVE (Diversify, Realize, Integrate, Venture, Educate) Science Centers are closer to unraveling a long-standing solar mystery surrounding the extreme thinness of the Sun’s tachocline layer, a region critical for creating space weather. The COFFIES (Consequences Of Fields and Flows in the Interior and Exterior of the Sun) center enabled […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NGC 6188: Dragons of Ara
entry.content:
Where can you find dragons fighting in the night sky?
In the southern constellation of the Altar: Ara
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
entry.content: Over the years, astronauts aboard the International Space Station have photographed several of the cities hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: HoRS3S
entry.content: Date: July 6, 2026 - July 10, 2026 Location: Granada, Spain The post HoRS3S appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Cartilage Engineering, Blood Pressure Studies Kick Off Week Aboard Station
entry.content: Cartilage engineering and cardiac research to advance human health on and off the Earth topped Monday’s research schedule aboard the International Space Station. The Expedition 74 crew members also continued spacesuit maintenance and cargo operations aboard the orbital outpost. The post Cartilage Engineering, Blood Pressure Studies Kick Off Week Aboard Station appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Telescope Milestone: NASA’s Roman Moves Vertical Ahead of Processing
entry.content: NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has completed important prelaunch milestones as it prepares to launch nine months ahead of schedule. Engineers at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida raised Roman from horizontal to vertical, signaling preparations are moving forward for upcoming inspections, functional testing, and integration work. Technicians transferred the observatory to NASA […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
entry.content: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover featuring his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. For 250 years, America has pushed the boundaries of what’s possible. From the earliest days of exploration, to the first steps on the Moon […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: ESA’s Euclid Space Telescope Finds Universe’s Most Ancient Quasars
entry.content: Led by ESA (European Space Agency) with contributions from NASA, the Euclid space telescope has discovered 31 of the oldest quasars ever documented. In fact, two are the oldest ever observed, dating back to the universe’s infancy, when it was just 5% of its current age. A new study details the results in the journal Astronomy & Astrophysics. A quasar occurs when immense amounts of gas and dust […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: XR SIG Seminar, 10 July 2026
entry.content: High Angular Resolution X-ray Imager (Hi-ReX) SAG Report on Extragalactic and Extreme Gravity Science.Speaker: Kimberly Weaver, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center The post XR SIG Seminar, 10 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
entry.content: On Monday, NASA released a draft Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking feedback from American companies on the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy, aimed at ensuring a seamless transition of activities in low Earth orbit from the International Space Station. “NASA’s review reflects what we’ve been hearing from industry throughout this process. Industry […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
entry.content: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated and advanced these capabilities. Designed to test and validate technologies in lunar orbit, CAPSTONE launched […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Strong Flare Erupts from Sun
entry.content: The Sun emitted a strong solar flare, peaking at 4:41 p.m. ET on July 4. NASA’s Solar Dynamics Observatory, which watches the Sun constantly, captured an image of the event. Solar flares are powerful bursts of energy. Flares and solar eruptions can impact radio communications, electric power grids, navigation signals, and pose risks to spacecraft […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
entry.content: In new images from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope to celebrate its fourth science anniversary, a familiar galaxy transforms into something far richer, and far more complex, than ever seen before. Webb’s unprecedented sensitivity across near- and mid-infrared wavelengths cuts through the thick lanes of dust that obscure Centaurus A’s center in visible light, showing […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Dueling Bands over the Atacama Desert
entry.content:
What are these two bands in the sky?
The more commonly seen band is on the left and is the central band of our Milky Way galaxy
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: IAUS 407: Origins 2026
entry.content: Date: July 6, 2026 - July 11, 2026 Location: Paris, France The post IAUS 407: Origins 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
entry.content: Using satellite data, researchers connected harmful algal blooms with warm water and low water levels at one of Colorado's largest reservoirs. The post Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Saturn's Iapetus: Painted Moon
entry.content:
What has happened to Saturn's moon Iapetus?
Vast sections of this strange world are dark as coal, while others are as bright as snow
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: SPIE Astronomical Telescopes + Instrumentation 2026
entry.content: Date: July 5, 2026 - July 10, 2026 Location: Copenhagen, Denmark The post SPIE Astronomical Telescopes + Instrumentation 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
entry.content: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Pathfinder on Mars
entry.content: On July 4th, 1997, using its own array of fireworks, a parachute, and a cocoon of airbags, the Mars Pathfinder spacecraft bounced like a giant beach ball at least 15 times before it came to rest on the surface of Mars at 10:07 AM Pacific Daylight Time
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Teams Make Contact With Spacecraft Set to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: Teams have successfully established communications with Katalyst Space’s robotic servicing spacecraft LINK.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Hubble Celebrates Nation’s 250th Birthday
entry.content: NASA's Hubble celebrates the nation's 250th birthday with new images and more! The post Hubble Celebrates Nation’s 250th Birthday appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TIC 150070085 b
entry.content: TIC 150070085 b is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 12.8 Earths, it takes 10.5 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.098439381424078 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TIC 150070085 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 126105 b
entry.content: HD 126105 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 1.67 Jupiters, it takes 1.4 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.36 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 126105 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 127195 c
entry.content: HD 127195 c is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 0.65 Jupiters, it takes 2.3 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.9162400865645 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 127195 c appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 127195 b
entry.content: HD 127195 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 0.67 Jupiters, it takes 1.5 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.4210244419444 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 127195 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 125136 b
entry.content: HD 125136 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 2.26 Jupiters, it takes 2.3 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 2.0319497862624 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 125136 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 190360 d
entry.content: HD 190360 d is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a G-type star. Its mass is 11.9 Earths, it takes 88.7 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.3886 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 190360 d appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: KMT-2023-BLG-0332L b
entry.content: KMT-2023-BLG-0332L b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a Unknown-type star. Its mass is 22 Jupiters, it takes 7.8 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 3.2 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post KMT-2023-BLG-0332L b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HIP 8923 b
entry.content: HIP 8923 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 9.98 Jupiters, it takes 14.1 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 5.9 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HIP 8923 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
entry.content: Blue and white stars shine brightly against crimson gas in this image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
entry.content: More than 500,000 stars blaze red, white, and blue in this NASA Hubble image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3).
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Three Galaxies in Pavo
entry.content: Some 190 million light-years away, far beyond the bright stars and nebulae of the Milky Way, these three galaxies are drawn together by gravity in a mesmerizing cosmic dance
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Mission To Boost NASA’s Swift Launches From Marshall Islands
entry.content: A mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory has reached orbit. The post Mission To Boost NASA’s Swift Launches From Marshall Islands appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: The Birthplace of the United States
entry.content: It was here the founders shaped the future of American government. The post The Birthplace of the United States appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA, Partners Update Launch Date for Mission to Boost Swift
entry.content: The launch of Katalyst's robotic servicing spacecraft LINK is now targeted for no earlier than Friday, July 3. The post NASA, Partners Update Launch Date for Mission to Boost Swift appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Amendment 61: A.4 Rapid Response and Novel Research in Earth Science Final Close Date Deferred
entry.content: A.4 Rapid Response and Novel Research in Earth Science (RRNES) solicits proposals that advance the goals and objectives of NASA’s Earth Science Division by conducting unique research to investigate 1) unforeseen or unpredictable Earth system events and opportunities that require a rapid response, and 2) novel ideas of potential high merit and relevance for ESD […] The post Amendment 61: A.4 Rapid Response and Novel Research in Earth Science Final Close Date Deferred appeared first on NASA Scienc
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Amendment 60: Final 2025 Due Date Extended for C.2 Solar System Science
entry.content: C.2 Solar System Science solicits proposals for research, data analysis, data preservation, and tools that support investigations to help ascertain the content, origin, and evolution of the Solar System and the search for life’s origin, evolution, distribution, and future in the universe. The Planetary Science Division is committed to supporting the most meritorious research, data […] The post Amendment 60: Final 2025 Due Date Extended for C.2 Solar System Science appeared first on NASA Science
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Collecting Lunar Rake Samples (Apollo 17)
entry.content: Photo of Harrison Schmitt collecting lunar rake samples during the first Apollo 17 extravehicular activity (EVA). The post Collecting Lunar Rake Samples (Apollo 17) appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Helio and You: Heliophysics as an American Endeavor
entry.content: Heliophysics is an important cornerstone of the history of astronomy, and many of the pioneers in this field have been American. This article examines a bit of this history in depth. The post Helio and You: Heliophysics as an American Endeavor appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
entry.content: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II mission mission drew unprecedented public interest – including more than 149.4 million views of the launch, lunar flyby, splashdown on NASA-owned platforms, including the 24/7 streams covering the mission and the Orion spacecraft views – demonstrating strong, sustained global engagement throughout the mission. Around the Clock Live Broadcast NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches to the Moon broadcast set unprecedented viewership records across the agency’s […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Students Get Airborne View of Atmospheric Science at Ellington Field
entry.content: From June 3 to 13, aircraft at Ellington Field in Houston gave students a firsthand look at how scientists study Earth from the air. Through NASA’s Student Airborne Research Program, or SARP, students learned how airborne field campaigns collect data used in atmospheric science, ecology, air quality research, and climate modeling. This year’s activity took place […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Cartilage, Cardiac Studies and Cargo Ops Wrap Up Week Aboard Station
entry.content: Cartilage repair, cardiac research, and cargo operations wrapped up the week for the Expedition 74 crew before heading into a relaxing three-day weekend. The International Space Station is also orbiting higher following a reboost maneuver on Wednesday.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Good Morning, Earth!
entry.content: NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16 sunrises and sunsets. Learn more about the orbiting laboratory. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
entry.content: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has captured the infrared light of numerous features that previously were impossible to see beyond the thick dust of the FS Tau star system. In addition to myriad background galaxies that burst into view like fireworks for the United States’ 250th anniversary celebrations, this image flickers with a number of […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Fourth of July Through Hubble’s Eyes
entry.content: To commemorate the nation's 250th birthday, Hubble shares 13 images for the nation's 13 original colonies. The post Fourth of July Through Hubble’s Eyes appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Launch of Swift Boost Mission Delayed, Teams Reviewing Data
entry.content: The launch of Katalyst’s robotic servicing spacecraft LINK on Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket was postponed Thursday.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Sibling Supernova Remnants
entry.content:
What happens when one of the stars in a binary goes supernova?
This image combines visible (yellow), ultraviolet (purple) and infrared light (cyan, red and orange) to show two supernova remnants and their surrounding environment, about 6,000 light-years away
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Colonial National Historical Park
entry.content: The colonial communities of “America’s historic triangle” played defining roles in the road to American independence. The post Colonial National Historical Park appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
entry.content: A predawn Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, a great chance to see the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings at a new angle. Skywatching Highlights Transcript An early morning hangout with the Moon and planets, a comet swings by, prime time for the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings shine at a new angle. That’s What’s Up […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Eyes software: June release notes
entry.content: A major update to Eyes on Exoplanets was released on June 11, 2026, complete with a brand-new tutorial. The introductory welcome screen was completely updated, and in a major upgrade, every single star in the app (4,716 stars and counting) now has its own description panel, similar to those of the exoplanets in the application. […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
entry.content: A new result using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory shows that the outer spiral arms in the Milky Way galaxy may reach wider than previously thought. This finding may lead astronomers to adjust their understanding of our home galaxy’s structure. A team of astronomers made this discovery by making precise measurements of distances to dust clouds […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: 2025 SBIR/STTR Ignite Phase I Welcome Webinar
entry.content: 2025 Ignite Phase I Welcome Webinar On July 1, 2026, the NASA SBIR/STTR program hosted a webinar to welcome the 2025 NASA SBIR and STTR Phase I awardees, who were selected in April 2026. The post 2025 SBIR/STTR Ignite Phase I Welcome Webinar appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Crew Splits Day Between Relaxing, Spacesuit Work, and Cardiac Research
entry.content: Four Expedition 74 astronauts had a light duty day on Wednesday following the previous day’s spacewalk for external robotics maintenance. The remaining three crew members from Roscosmos focused on cardiac research and lab maintenance aboard the International Space Station.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
entry.content: NASA is recruiting research participants for the agency’s next simulated deep space mission. Beginning no earlier than August 2027, research volunteers will spend one year living and working in interplanetary environments at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, operating under isolated conditions expected during crewed missions to the Moon or Red Planet. Insights from this […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for Yearlong Simulated Mission to Moon, Mars
entry.content: NASA Seeks Volunteers for Yearlong Simulated Mission to Moon, Mars NASA is recruiting participants for the Moon and Mars Exploration Analog mission, set to begin no earlier than August 2027 at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. For one year, research volunteers will live and work in isolation and confinement while simulating interplanetary transit and […] The post NASA Seeks Volunteers for Yearlong Simulated Mission to Moon, Mars appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Lindsey Wade
entry.content: Affiliation– NASA Ames Research Center Division– Human Systems Integration Division (TH) Email– lindsey.e.wade@nasa.gov The post Lindsey Wade appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Data Helps CDC Track Air Quality During World Cup 2026
entry.content: NASA air quality information is supporting CDC planning as World Cup crowds gather across North America.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
entry.content: A first-of-its-kind mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory is poised for launch no earlier than Thursday, July 2, 5:09 a.m. EDT (9:09 p.m. UTC+12), from Kwajalein Atoll, part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands in the South Pacific Ocean. A robotic servicing spacecraft called LINK, built by Katalyst Space, will blast […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star
entry.content: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope is giving us new insight into the far-future of solar systems like our own, as the agency continues to reveal the secrets of the universe and our place in it. Billions of years ago, a Sun-like star nearing the end of its life swelled tremendously in size to become a […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4934-4940: In the Land of the Polygons
entry.content: Written by William Farrand, Senior Research Scientist, Space Science Institute Earth planning date: Friday, June 26, 2026 There were two planning cycles over this span of sols. The Monday planning took place with Curiosity situated within a unit that from orbital imagery appeared light-toned, and from earlier rover positions appeared smooth. Reaching this unit, the […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: A-BOX
entry.content: Astromaterials Glovebox Systems – Design & Fabrication (A-BOX) Points of Contact Name: Victoria N. MillerTitle: Contracting OfficerEmail: victoria.n.miller@nasa.gov Name: Ealena TelsonTitle: Contract SpecialistEmail: ealena.telson@nasa.gov Source Selection Authority (SSA): Stephen H. Janney Streamlined Procurement Team (SLPT): Andrea D. Harrington, Chairperson Questions and Answers Electronic responses are through sam.gov Current Information Sources Sought Synopsis and Related Documents […] The
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Caltech Welcomes Astrophysicist Ray Jayawardhana as New President
entry.content: Ray Jayawardhana begins his tenure today as the 10th president of the California Institute of Technology. His selection as Caltech’s president, and as the Sonja and William Davidow Presidential Chair and professor of astronomy, was announced Jan. 6. Jayawardhana succeeds Thomas Rosenbaum, who had served as Caltech’s president since 2014. Founded in 1891, Caltech manages […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s TESS Mission Finds Planetary System in New Way
entry.content: For the first time, NASA’s TESS (Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite) mission has identified a planet orbiting a distant star thanks to ripples in space-time. Unlike the star-hugging transiting planets TESS regularly reveals, the newfound world is a super-Jupiter orbiting far from its host star. “When TESS launched, no one expected it to ever be capable […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: The Cotton Candy Clouds of Rho Ophiuchi
entry.content: Although they look like cotton candy, you cannot eat these clouds! Taken in Cádiz, Spain, today's image features the Rho Ophiuchi complex, a rich tapestry of young and old astronomical phenomena
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Weather Delays Launch of Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: The launch of Katalyst Space’s LINK, designed to boost the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory, was postponed again Wednesday due to unfavorable weather conditions. The post Weather Delays Launch of Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Minute Man National Historical Park
entry.content: Sites relevant to the start of the American Revolutionary War are interspersed throughout the modern-day Boston metropolitan region. The post Minute Man National Historical Park appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Sun Releases Strong Flare
entry.content: The Sun emitted a strong solar flare, peaking at 4:50 p.m. ET on June 30. NASA’s Solar Dynamics Observatory, which watches the Sun constantly, captured an image of the event. Solar flares are powerful bursts of energy. Flares and solar eruptions can impact radio communications, electric power grids, navigation signals, and pose risks to spacecraft […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Digital Flight Rerouting Capability Tech Transfer
entry.content: Digital Flight Rerouting Capability The Air Traffic Management and Safety (ATMS) Project, as part of NASA’s Airspace Operations and Safety Program (AOSP), developed and evaluated the Digital Flight Rerouting Capability. The team is transferring the capability to the FAA and airline partners for further evaluation, system integration, procedure development, and implementation. Today, when bad weather […] The post Digital Flight Rerouting Capability Tech Transfer appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Chandra Reveals ‘Red, White, Blue’ Universe for US 250th
entry.content: In celebration of the 250th birthday of the United States, NASA has unveiled four cosmic images from its Chandra X-ray Observatory rendered in red, white, and blue that represent the wonders of the universe the agency explores. The images are accompanied by a trio of new sonifications – a technique that translates astronomical data into […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Astronauts Repair Canadarm2 Robotic Arm and Complete Spacewalk
entry.content: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir concluded their spacewalk outside the International Space Station at 3:40 p.m. EDT. During the 7-hour, 20-minute spacewalk, Williams and Meir completed their primary objective to remove and replace a wrist joint on the Canadarm2 robotic arm. The post Astronauts Repair Canadarm2 Robotic Arm and Complete Spacewalk appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: DGCE SIG Seminar, 23 July 2026
entry.content: From Micro to Macro: Connecting the Physics of Galaxy Formation Across Scales. Speaker: Drummond Fielding, New York University The post DGCE SIG Seminar, 23 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: A Day of Flight Testing at NASA Armstrong
entry.content: Flight testing is a team sport. For nearly 80 years, teams at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, have used flight testing to push the limits of aerodynamics and advance aviation. Earlier this year, NASA’s Crossflow Attenuated Natural Laminar Flow (CATNLF) initiative tested a wing concept that would maximize the smooth flow of […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: La NASA adjudica nuevas misiones científicas para Base Lunar y adelanta nuevas oportunidades
entry.content: Read this news release in English here. Nota del editor: Este comunicado se actualizó el 30 de junio de 2026 para aclarar la versión de desarrollo de ingeniería del rover PROMISE.La NASA anunció el martes la selección de tres empresas para llevar a cabo cuatro nuevas misiones a la Luna a finales de 2028 como […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NextSTEP-3 B: Moon Base Demonstrations
entry.content: Notice ID: Coming Soon NASA’s Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate is seeking innovative ideas from industry partners through a new solicitation appendix under the NextSTEP-3 Omnibus Broad Agency Announcement. Appendix B: Moon Base Demonstrations calls for industry-led demonstrations, risk reduction, and special topic activities that enable an enduring human presence on the lunar surface. NASA’s Moon […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Awards More Moon Base Science, Previews New Opportunities
entry.content: Editor’s note: This release was updated on June 30, 2026, to clarify the engineering development version for the PROMISE rover. NASA announced Tuesday the selection of three companies to land four new missions on the Moon in late 2028 as part of the agency’s Moon Base Program. Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines will deliver […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Texas Aerospace Scholars Senior Experience
entry.content: Overview The TAS Senior Experience is a six-month commitment for high school seniors in which students engage directly with NASA scientists and engineers while learning about the relationship between manufacturing and the engineering design process. The Senior Experience begins with Ignition, an online course where students explore topics in aerospace manufacturing, engineering, and other areas […] The post Texas Aerospace Scholars Senior Experience appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Fireflies and Fireballs: Summer Stargazing with Meteor Watch Day, Asteroid Day
entry.content: We’re at the onset of summer skywatching, with a double feature of the Southern Delta Aquariids and alpha Capricornids in July and the dazzling Perseids approaching in August. Before we step outside to enjoy the celestial fireworks, we’re kicking off the season by observing Meteor Watch Day and Asteroid Day, both today, June 30. Meteors […] The post Fireflies and Fireballs: Summer Stargazing with Meteor Watch Day, Asteroid Day appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Spacewalkers Continuing Canadarm2 Robotic Arm Repairs
entry.content: Spacewalkers Chris Williams and Jessica Meir successfully removed the wrist joint that malfunctioned on May 27 during Canadarm2 robotic arm operations. They now are working to install the replacement joint, which was already attached to the orbital complex. The post Spacewalkers Continuing Canadarm2 Robotic Arm Repairs appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: A Most “Pleasantly Situated” Estate
entry.content: The farms and gardens of Mount Vernon played a central role in the life of America’s first president.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: CAPSTONE 02
entry.content: Small Spacecraft & Distributed Systems (SSDS)HomeAboutTechnology DemonstrationsPartnershipsWork with UsResourcesS3VI CAPSTONE 02 Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment Future Mission NASA’s CAPSTONE 02 mission will demonstrate rendezvous and proximity operations, autonomous navigation, and cislunar communication capabilities while continuing to characterize the radiation environment at the Moon. Short for Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Tech
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Starry Chandelier Cluster
entry.content: This image released on June 26, 2026, features the globular cluster NGC 6723, sometimes called the Chandelier Cluster. Like its namesake, it sparkles with countless lights. However, each ‘lightbulb’ in this chandelier is an individual star 27,000 light-years away in the constellation Sagittarius (the Archer). Globular clusters like NGC 6723 contain some of the oldest […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Reminder: ASTRA Mission Concepts & Ad ASTRA Workshop Abstracts
entry.content: Just a reminder that tomorrow, Wednesday 1 July 2026, is the deadline for submitting Astrophysics Strategic Technology & Research Accelerator (ASTRA) mission concepts and also for submitting abstracts for the Ad ASTRA Workshop. The post Reminder: ASTRA Mission Concepts & Ad ASTRA Workshop Abstracts appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: AMMG STIG Meeting, 30 June 2026
entry.content: We will be holding our AMMB SIG meeting today (6/30) at 9:00am PDT. Connection information below. A meeting recording will be shared for those unable to attend live. The post AMMG STIG Meeting, 30 June 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Astronauts Begin Spacewalk to Repair Canadarm2 Robotic Arm
entry.content: NASA astronauts Chris Williams and Jessica Meir began a spacewalk at 8:20 a.m. EDT today to replace a wrist joint that malfunctioned during normal Canadarm2 operations on May 27. The post Astronauts Begin Spacewalk to Repair Canadarm2 Robotic Arm appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Astronauts Prepare to Exit Station for Robotics Repair Spacewalk
entry.content: Live coverage is underway as two NASA astronauts prepare for a spacewalk outside the International Space Station. The spacewalk is scheduled to begin at about 8:35 a.m. EDT and last roughly six and a half hours. The post Astronauts Prepare to Exit Station for Robotics Repair Spacewalk appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Unusually Smooth Sections of Asteroid Itokawa
entry.content:
Why are parts of this asteroid's surface so smooth?
The answer seems likely to do with the dynamics of an asteroid that is a loose pile of rubble rather than a solid rock
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Launch of Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Scrubs Due to Weather
entry.content: The launch of Katalyst Space’s LINK designed to boost the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory, was postponed Tuesday due to unfavorable weather conditions. The post Launch of Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Scrubs Due to Weather appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Ames Science Stars of the Month July 2026
entry.content: NASA Ames Science Stars of the Month: July 2026 The NASA Ames Science Directorate recognizes the outstanding contributions of (pictured left to right) Sungshin Choi, Yi-Chun Chen, Emma Yates, Eduardo Bendek. Their commitment to the NASA mission represents the entrepreneurial spirit, technical expertise, and collaborative disposition needed to explore this world and beyond. Space Biosciences […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Laboratory Astrophysics Newsletter Issue 3
entry.content: Lab Astro NewsletterLab Astro Newsletter HomeIssuesIssue 1: Cold Solar System ObjectsIssue 2: ExoplanetsIssue 3: Astrophysical IcesInterviewsContribute The post Laboratory Astrophysics Newsletter Issue 3 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Viking Mission Resources
entry.content: A collection of images, videos, activities, and other downloadable resources about NASA's Viking 1 and 2 missions to Mars. The post Viking Mission Resources appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways Project Celebrates Student Innovation Through ROADS from Earth to Venus National Challenge
entry.content: The Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways (NESSP) project recently concluded its 2025–2026 ROADS (Rover Observation And Discoveries in Space) from Earth to Venus National Challenge, a NASA Science Activation program student challenge that engaged more than 500 students on 120 teams from eight states in authentic science and engineering experiences inspired by Venus exploration. […] The post Northwest Earth and Space Science Pathways Project Celebrates Student Innovation Through ROADS from E
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: What to Expect: Commercial Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: A mission to boost the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory is targeted to launch no earlier than Tuesday, June 30, at 10:17 p.m. UTC+12 (6:17 a.m. EDT). The post What to Expect: Commercial Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Scientific American’s Inaugural ‘Young American Scientists’ Recognition
entry.content: Scientific American’s “Young American Scientists” is a new annual recognition program honoring early- and mid-career researchers whose work is advancing discovery, expanding knowledge, and helping shape the future of science. The post Scientific American’s Inaugural ‘Young American Scientists’ Recognition appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.content: Unable to render the provided source The post Lunar Development and Test Facility appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.title: More Research and Technology News
entry.content: More Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD) News Use this page to stay up-to-date with the latest news about NASA’s Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD). ARMD Research Solicitations (Updated June 23) 9 min read THIS PAGE WAS UPDATED ON JUNE 23, 2026 This Aeronautics Research Mission Directorate (ARMD) solicitations page compiles the opportunities to… […] The post More Research and Technology News appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: ROSES-25 A.12 HAMAQ Science Team Clarified
entry.content: Hemispheric Airborne Measurements of Air Quality (HAMAQ) is a NASA-funded project under the EVS-4 program using two aircraft (NASA’s B777 & P-3B) to sample in coordination with satellites (like TEMPO) and surface observations to develop a multi-perspective view of air quality conditions useful for challenging and improving models, see https://science.larc.nasa.gov/hamaq Program element A.12 HAMAQ Science […] The post ROSES-25 A.12 HAMAQ Science Team Clarified appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA, SBA Announce New Initiative to Scale American Space Economy
entry.content: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) launched the SBIC-NASA Initiative on Monday to increase investment in American manufacturers of industrial components and providers of technologies critical to space exploration to support a sustained presence on the Moon and Mars. Under the Memorandum of Agreement, NASA will identify technology priorities and connect businesses to funding opportunities […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Newest Wind Tunnel Builds on Legacy of Innovation
entry.content: For more than 100 years, wind tunnels at NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, have helped shape the future of flight. Now, two of NASA’s longest-serving facilities — the 12-Foot Low-Speed Tunnel and the 20-Foot Vertical Spin Tunnel — will pass the torch to the Flight Dynamics Research Facility (FDRF), the first major NASA wind tunnel built in more than 40 years. “The FDRF […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Structures and Materials
entry.content: Structures and Materials Branches Branch Leadership Advanced Materials and Processing Chris Wohl, Branch Head Durability, Damage Tolerance, and Reliability Frank Leone, Branch Head Ryan Norman, Assistant Branch Head Structural Mechanics and Concepts Adam Przekop, Branch Head Marc Schultz, Acting Assistant Branch Head Nondestructive Evaluation Sciences Jason Moore, Branch Head Structural Dynamics Matthew Chamberlain, Branch Head […] The post Structures and Materials appeared first on NASA Science

entry.title: Intelligent Flight Systems
entry.content: Intelligent Flight Systems Branches Branch Leadership Vehicle Dynamics and Control Oscar Murillo, Branch HeadCorey Diebler, Assistant Branch Head Crew Systems & Aviation Operations Lisa Le Vie, Branch Head Safety-Critical Avionics Systems Aaron Dutie, Branch Head Revolutionary Aviation Technologies Scott Luers, Branch Head Autonomous Integrated Systems Research Trey Arthur, Branch HeadJohn Cooper, Acting Assistant Branch Head The post Intelligent Flight Systems appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.title: Aerosciences
entry.content: Aerosciences Branches Branch Leadership Configuration Aerodynamics David Chan, Branch Head Computational AeroSciences David Lockard, Branch Head Advanced Measurements & Flow Physics Luther Jenkins, Branch Head Shelly Sadowski, Assistant Branch Head Aerothermodynamics Kelly Murphy, Branch Head Bill Wood, Assistant Branch Head Hypersonic Airbreathing Propulsion Richard Gaffney, Branch Head Robin Hunt, Assistant Branch Head Aeroelasticity David Piatak, […] The post Aerosciences appeared first on NA

entry.title: Research and Technology Mission Directorate
entry.content: Research and Technology Mission Directorate The Research and Technology Mission Directorate develops and delivers breakthroughs in aeronautics, space technology, and nuclear power and propulsion to ensure American leadership in aerospace, along with the communications and navigation systems that are critical to every NASA mission. Learn More About RTMD about Research and Technology Mission Directorate RTMD […] The post Research and Technology Mission Directorate appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Astronauts Ready for Tuesday Spacewalk to Repair Canadarm2 Robotic Arm
entry.content: The Expedition 74 astronauts are ready for a spacewalk on Tuesday following the completion of spacesuit configurations and procedure reviews on Monday. The International Space Station’s three cosmonauts kept busy throughout the day servicing Roscosmos scientific, electronics, and life support systems.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD) Leadership
entry.content: Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD) Leadership A listing of the leadership for NASA’s Research and Technology Mission Directorate and its divisions with links to their biographies. Learn More About NASA’s RTMD about Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD) Leadership Office of the Associate Administrator for Research and Technology Research and Technology Mission Directorate Leadership […] The post Research and Technology Mission Directorate (RTMD) Leadership appeared fi

entry.title: NASA Office of Strategic Capital
entry.content: In partnership with the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA), NASA’s Office of Strategic Capital (NOSC) is launching a multi-year initiative to strengthen the commercial space supply chain. Building on the SBA’s proven Small Business Investment Company framework, the partnership channels a combination of public and private capital to American-owned small businesses on which NASA’s missions […] The post NASA Office of Strategic Capital appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate
entry.content: Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate The Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate leads NASA’s human spaceflight and space-enabling activities across low Earth orbit, the Moon, and beyond. The directorate provides unified leadership for the agency’s systems, operations, and architectures to deliver on the next era of human spaceflight. Directorate Leadership Dr. Lori GlazeAssociate AdministratorRead MoreJoel R. MontalbanoDeputy Associate […] The post Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate appeared
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Call for Input: Assessment of Technical & Scientific Capabilities at NASA GSFC
entry.content: The National Academies are conducting an assessment of the technical and scientific capabilities at NASA Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC). The post Call for Input: Assessment of Technical & Scientific Capabilities at NASA GSFC appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Call for Experts: Review of Progress Toward the Astro2020 Decadal Survey
entry.content: The National Academies are seeking suggestions for experts to participate in a new study to review the responses of NASA's Astrophysics program, NSF's Astronomy program, and DOE's Cosmic Frontiers program to recommendations from the decadal survey. The post Call for Experts: Review of Progress Toward the Astro2020 Decadal Survey appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Call for Reviewers: MUREP Space Technology Artemis Research
entry.content: NASA Research and Education Support Services (NRESS) is seeking individuals willing to serve as peer reviewers for the NASA MUREP Space Technology Artemis Research (M-STAR) opportunity. The post Call for Reviewers: MUREP Space Technology Artemis Research appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science
